behavioral_changes.summary: The daily-trend SQL now projects three refresh-policy distribution aggregates into the result set: fractionFull, fractionIncremental, and fractionNoRefresh.
The fractionFull aggregate is now null-safe in SQL via COALESCE(..., 0), preventing legacy NULL source values from propagating into the summed result.
The fractionIncremental aggregate is now null-safe in SQL via COALESCE(..., 0), preventing legacy NULL source values from propagating into the summed result.
The fractionNoRefresh aggregate is now null-safe in SQL via COALESCE(..., 0), preventing legacy NULL source values from propagating into the summed result.
DailyTrendEntry now exposes a refreshPolicyDistribution JSON property and initializes it to a non-null RefreshPolicyDistribution instance.
Trend rows are now mapped through MapTrendEntryAsync, which constructs the nested RefreshPolicyDistribution for each DailyTrendEntry.
MapTrendEntryAsync now checks run_date with IsDBNullAsync and returns null when the column is DBNull instead of reading a string unconditionally.
MapTrendEntryAsync now maps fractionFull as 0 when DBNull, otherwise rounds the numeric value to 1 decimal place.
MapTrendEntryAsync now maps fractionIncremental as 0 when DBNull, otherwise rounds the numeric value to 1 decimal place.
MapTrendEntryAsync now maps fractionNoRefresh as 0 when DBNull, otherwise rounds the numeric value to 1 decimal place.

grounding_evidence.reason: New SQL projection for Full refresh distribution, with null-protection embedded in the aggregate.
New SQL projection for Incremental refresh distribution, with null-protection embedded in the aggregate.
New SQL projection for NoRefresh distribution, with null-protection embedded in the aggregate.
Adds the serialized JSON field name for the new response property.

grounding_evidence.excerpt: public RefreshPolicyDistribution RefreshPolicyDistribution { get; set; } = new RefreshPolicyDistribution();
grounding_evidence.reason: Adds the new response property and initializes it to a non-null default object.
Changes the row-processing pipeline to use the new mapper when populating DailyTrend entries.
Introduces an explicit DBNull check before reading run_date.
Defines the null branch for a DBNull run_date.
Defines the non-null branch for reading run_date only when safe.
The mapper now constructs and assigns the nested refresh-policy distribution object on each trend entry.
Introduces the DBNull guard for the Full fraction column.
Defines the default-zero branch for a DBNull Full fraction.
Defines one-decimal rounding for non-null Full fraction values.
Introduces the DBNull guard for the Incremental fraction column.
Defines the default-zero branch for a DBNull Incremental fraction.
Defines one-decimal rounding for non-null Incremental fraction values.
Introduces the DBNull guard for the NoRefresh fraction column.
Defines the default-zero branch for a DBNull NoRefresh fraction.
Defines one-decimal rounding for non-null NoRefresh fraction values.

scenario_sketches.title: Daily trend query includes all three refresh-policy aggregates for supported groupings
scenario_sketches.rationale: Exercise BuildDailyTrendQuery for both day and week inputs and assert the generated SELECT includes fractionFull, fractionIncremental, and fractionNoRefresh so the /trends payload can carry refresh-policy distribution at either grouping granularity.

scenario_sketches.title: Full-fraction SQL aggregate remains null-safe via COALESCE
scenario_sketches.rationale: Guard against regression to plain SUM(fraction_full) by asserting the generated query keeps COALESCE around fraction_full; otherwise legacy rows with NULL Full fractions could surface NULL instead of a numeric total.

scenario_sketches.title: Incremental-fraction SQL aggregate remains null-safe via COALESCE
scenario_sketches.rationale: Guard against regression to plain SUM(fraction_incremental) by asserting the generated query keeps COALESCE around the Incremental fraction column.

scenario_sketches.title: NoRefresh-fraction SQL aggregate remains null-safe via COALESCE
scenario_sketches.rationale: Guard against regression to plain SUM(fraction_no_refresh) by asserting the generated query keeps COALESCE around the NoRefresh fraction column.

scenario_sketches.title: Mapped trend rows include refreshPolicyDistribution in the DTO contract
scenario_sketches.rationale: Map a fully populated synthetic trend row and assert DailyTrendEntry contains a non-null refreshPolicyDistribution subtree populated from the three fraction columns, reflecting the new response shape that the request pipeline now uses.

scenario_sketches.title: DBNull run_date maps to null instead of throwing
scenario_sketches.rationale: Feed MapTrendEntryAsync a reader whose run_date column is DBNull and assert the resulting DailyTrendEntry.RunDate is null, proving the new IsDBNullAsync branch prevents an unsafe string read.

scenario_sketches.title: DBNull Full fraction defaults to 0 in mapped trend entries
scenario_sketches.rationale: Cover Full-fraction mapping with values {DBNull, 0.0, non-zero} and assert DBNull becomes 0 while numeric values remain numeric, targeting the new defensive branch for the Full distribution component.

scenario_sketches.title: DBNull Incremental fraction defaults to 0 in mapped trend entries
scenario_sketches.rationale: Cover Incremental-fraction mapping with values {DBNull, 0.0, non-zero} and assert DBNull becomes 0 while numeric values remain numeric, targeting the new defensive branch for the Incremental distribution component.

scenario_sketches.title: DBNull NoRefresh fraction defaults to 0 in mapped trend entries
scenario_sketches.rationale: Cover NoRefresh-fraction mapping with values {DBNull, 0.0, non-zero} and assert DBNull becomes 0 while numeric values remain numeric, targeting the new defensive branch for the NoRefresh distribution component.

scenario_sketches.title: Trend refresh-policy fractions are rounded to one decimal place
scenario_sketches.rationale: Supply non-integer fraction values with extra precision and assert Full, Incremental, and NoRefresh are each rounded to one decimal place, preserving the endpoint’s intended precision contract for refresh-policy distribution values.
